data.title: BOE Faces Pressure to Scale Back QT Programme After Gilts Selloff — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of England could come under pressure to scale back its quantitative easing programme if U.K. government bond yields continue to rise, MUFG Bank's Lee Hardman says in a note. The BOE plans to reduce its gilt holdings by GBP100 billion in a 12-month period from October 2024, including GBP12 billion through active gilt sales and the rest allowing gilts to mature. The selloff in gilts looks mainly driven by a broader repricing of long-end bonds rather than U.K. specific factors, Hardman says. "Having said that the rising cost of U.K. government borrowing if sustained will put pressure on the government to tighten fiscal policy."(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Currencies drift as dollar, weak output keep up pressure
data.description:
Central Europe's currencies struggled for direction on Thursday, hemmed in by a still strong U.S. dollar and weak local industrial output data.

Most currencies in the region have remained under pressure at the start of 2025, and a Reuters poll on Thursday forecast slight losses for the coming year on lingering economic risks, including potential U.S. tariffs.

Data on Thursday showed industrial output falling more than expected in the Czech Republic and Hungary in November.

The Czech crown EURCZK was little changed at 25.12 to the euro at 1021 GMT while Hungary's forint EURHUF had also barely moved at 413.90 to the euro, hovering near Monday's two-year low of 416.50.

"(The forint) saw some relief yesterday, but we see little chance of this being the start of a broader rally," ING said in a trading note, adding that more closures of sizeable short forint positions could generate positive momentum.

"However, we don't see much reason for the market to move in that direction at the moment given that the consensus seems to be heading for higher EUR/HUF levels later," the bank said.

A Reuters poll forecast the forint would fall to 420 per euro by the end of the year. The currency dropped more than 6% in 2024.

Poland's zloty (EURPLN) eased 0.1% to 4.2785 per euro, after moving beyond 4.25 at the start of the week, its strongest since 2020.

The Polish finance ministry sold a series of bonds on Thursday, as yields remain elevated.

A 10-year benchmark bond (PL10YT=RR) was bid around 6%, around its highest yield since October and compared with around 5% midway through 2024.

Thursday's bond sale was taking place in "rather unfavourable circumstances", Alior Bank said, adding that the zloty was also suffering from increased risk aversion and a stronger dollar.

The stronger dollar has weighed on central Europe's currencies, with market focus on U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's agenda as he steps back into the White House on Jan. 20. Analysts expected his policies to bolster U.S. growth and add to price pressures.

data.title: Parkson Retail Unit Renews Tenancy Contract
data.description:
Parkson Retail Group 3368 unit Liupanshui Parkson Retail renewed an agreement to continue the tenancy of a China property leased from Guizhou Hanhua Real Estate Development for 10 years, a Thursday bourse filing said.

The current lease is expiring Dec. 31, 2026, and the new 10-year contract will take effect from Jan. 1, 2027, according to the store, mall, and supermarket operator.

The property comprises parts of Baolong International Building in Liupanshui City, Guizhou Province, China, and is being used by the company for the operation of department stores since 2012.

data.title: EU Regulator OKs Smartsheet's Acquisition by Vista Equity Partners, Blackstone
data.description:
The European Commission greenlit the joint acquisition of Frankfurt-listed software company Smartsheet SMAR by US investors Vista Equity Partners Management and Blackstone.

The European Union watchdog said Thursday that the transaction, which relates to the collaborative work management software sector, is not expected to raise competition concerns due to the companies' limited combined market position after the merger.

data.title: Teleflex awarded Short Term Central Venous Catheters contract from Vizient
data.description:
WAYNE, Pa., Jan. 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Teleflex Incorporated TFX, a leading global provider of medical technologies, today announced it has been awarded a contract by Vizient, Inc., the largest provider-driven healthcare performance improvement company in the U.S. The agreement, effective January 1, 2025, covers the supply of Teleflex’s Central Venous Access Catheters and Arterial Catheters.

Vizient provides solutions and services that improve the delivery of high-value care by aligning cost, quality, and market performance for more than 65% of the nation’s acute care providers. This includes 97% of the nation’s academic medical centers, and more than 35% of the acute care market. Vizient provides expertise, analytics, and advisory services, as well as a contract portfolio that represents more than $140 billion in annual purchasing volume to improve patient outcomes and lower costs. Through this agreement, Vizient provider-customers will now gain access to increased savings and pre-negotiated terms on Teleflex’s vascular access products.

“As the market leader in both Central Venous Catheters (CVCs)1 and Arterials2, we are pleased to offer Vizient provider-customers one of the broadest portfolios of vascular access products,” said Lisa Kudlacz, President and General Manager, Teleflex Vascular. “Arrow™ Vascular Access Products are designed to equally benefit clinicians and patients, help protect against vascular access related complications like infection, thrombosis, and tip malposition, and help clinicians follow independent third-party vascular access guidelines3-6, 8-13.”

For more than four decades, Teleflex, through its Arrow™ Catheters has been at the forefront of vascular access innovation. The Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVCs feature broad-spectrum antimicrobial protection against fungi, gram-positive, and gram-negative bacteria, reducing Central Line-Associated Bloodstream Infections (CLABSI) by 67-100%3-6.

The Catheter-Related Bloodstream Infection (CRBSI) risk rates for Arterial Catheters and short-term CVCs have been shown to be comparable, indicating that Arterials should receive the same precautions for catheter insertion and care.7 Arrow™ Arterial Catheterization Kits provide the essential tools and safety features in an all-inclusive kit—with a layout that is intuitive and easy to use.

The contract includes access to a range of products, including:

  • Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVCs
  • Arrow™ ErgoPack™ Complete Systems
  • Arrow™ Arterial Catheters and Catheterization Kits

These solutions help healthcare systems:

  • Comply with practice recommendations including: CDC Recommendations, SHEA Guidelines, INS Standards of Practice and OSHA Bloodborne Pathogens Standard8-11
  • Standardize vascular access products across a healthcare system
  • Maintain a high standard of patient care
  • Control costs and risks from infections3-6, 12

To learn more about Arrow™ Antimicrobial Catheter and Kitting Solutions, visit our website.

About Teleflex Incorporated

As a global provider of medical technologies, Teleflex is driven by our purpose to improve the health and quality of people’s lives. Through our vision to become the most trusted partner in healthcare, we offer a diverse portfolio with solutions in the therapy areas of anesthesia, emergency medicine, interventional cardiology and radiology, surgical, vascular access, and urology. We believe that the potential of great people, purpose driven innovation, and world-class products can shape the future direction of healthcare.

Teleflex is the home of Arrow™, Barrigel™, Deknatel™, LMA™, Pilling™, QuikClot™, Rüsch™, UroLift™ and Weck™ – trusted brands united by a common sense of purpose.

At Teleflex, we are empowering the future of healthcare. For more information, please visit teleflex.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Any statements contained in this press release that do not describe historical facts may constitute forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statements contained herein are based on our management's current beliefs and expectations, but are subject to a number of risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances, which may cause actual results or company actions to differ materially from what is expressed or implied by these statements. These risks and uncertainties are identified and described in more detail in our filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including our Annual Report on Form 10-K.

References

  • IQIVA Data. Data based on 2024 Q3 Total Acute CVC market unit share for Teleflex. 2024.
  • IQVIA Data. Data based on 2024 Q3 Total Arterial market unit share for Teleflex. 2024.
  • Rupp ME, Lisco SJ, Lipsett PA, et al. Effect of a Second-Generation Venous Catheter Impregnated with Chlorhexidine and Silver Sulfadiazine of Central Catheter-Related Infections. Ann Intern Medicine. 2005; 143: 570-80. Sponsored by Arrow (Teleflex).
  • Lorente L, Lecuona M, Jimenez A, et al. Chlorhexidine-silver sulfadiazine-impregnated venous catheters save costs. American Journal of Infection Control. 2014; 42: 321-4.
  • Lorente L, Lecuona M, Jimenez A, et al. Cost/benefit analysis of chlorhexidine-silver sulfadiazine-impregnated venous catheters for femoral access. American Journal of Infection Control. 2014; 42: 1130-2.
  • Lorente, L, et al. Chlorhexidine-silver sulfadiazine-impregnated venous catheters are efficient even at subclavian sites without tracheostomy. American journal of infection control. 2016; 44(12): 1526-29. Zimlichman, E, et al.
  • Lucet et al. Infectious risk associated with arterial catheters compared to central venous catheters. Society of Critical Care Medicine. 2010 Vol.38, No. 4.
  • O’Grady NP, Alexander M, Burns LA, et al. Guidelines for the Prevention of Intravascular Catheter-Related Infections, 2011 (Revised 2017). 2. Atlanta, GA: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention; 2017.
  • Buetti, N., Marschall, J., Drees, M., Fakih, M., Hadaway, L., Maragakis, L., Mermel, L. (2022). Strategies to prevent central line-associated bloodstream infections in acute-care hospitals: 2022 Update. Infection Control & Hospital Epidemiology, 1-17. doi:10.1017/ice.2022.87.
  • Nickel B, Gorski LA, Kleidon TM, et al. Infusion therapy standards of practice. J InfusNurs. 2024;47(suppl1):S1-S285.
  • Occupational Safety & Health Administration Regulations (Standards – 29 CFR). Part 1910.1030: Bloodborne pathogens. Occupational Safety & Health Administration Web site. https://www.osha.gov/laws-regs/regulations/standardnumber/1910/1910.1030. Accessed on February 12, 2020.
  • Abouleish, YZ, Oldfield EC, Marik PE. Comparison of central-line–associated bloodstream infections between central venous catheters lined by combined chlorhexidine and silver sulfadiazine versus silver ionotrophes alone: A before–after–before retrospective study. Infection Control & Hospital Epidemiology (2020): 1-3.
  • As compared to uncoated PICCs, intravascular ovine model inoculated with Staph aureus: AVAR-000427. No correlation between in vitro/in vivo testing methods and clinical outcomes have currently been ascertained.

Rx only

Contraindication: The Arrowg+ard Blue Plus™ CVC is contraindicated for patients with known hypersensitivity to chlorhexidine and silver sulfadiazine and/or sulfa drugs.

data.title: Apogee Enterprises Raises Quarterly Dividend to 26c From 25c >APOG
Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend >APOG
Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
(11:30 GMT) *Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend >APOG
(11:30 GMT) Press Release: Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend

Apogee Enterprises Increases Quarterly Dividend

Apogee's Twelfth Consecutive Year with a Dividend Increase

MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--January 09, 2025--

Apogee Enterprises, Inc. APOG announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.26 per share, a 4% increase from its previous quarterly dividend rate of $0.25 per share. The dividend will be payable on February 12, 2025, to shareholders of record at the close of business on January 28, 2025. This marks Apogee's twelfth consecutive year with a dividend increase, during which time the quarterly dividend has grown by 189%, from $0.09 per share to $0.26 per share.

About Apogee Enterprises, Inc.

Apogee Enterprises, Inc. APOG is a leading provider of architectural building products and services, as well as high-performance coated materials used in a variety of applications. Headquartered in Minneapolis, MN, our portfolio of industry-leading products and services includes architectural glass, windows, curtainwall, storefront and entrance systems, integrated project management and installation services, and high-performance coatings that provide protection, innovative design, and enhanced performance. For more information, visit www.apog.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250109089963/en/

CONTACT: Jeff Huebschen

Vice President, Investor Relations & Communications

952.487.7538

ir@apog.com

data.title: Riber Bags New Order from Teledyne Imaging Sensors
data.description:
Riber ALRIB said Thursday it secured a repeat order for its MBE 412 cluster system from Teledyne Imaging Sensors in the US.

The contract will help Teledyne expand its production capacity to fulfill commitments for advanced devices for infrared cameras used in space and terrestrial astronomy.

The latest order follows one placed by Teledyne in July 2024, the French semiconductor equipment maker said.

data.title: COMMENT-Multiple reasons why EUR/USD will likely drop to parity
data.description:
There is a combination of seasonal, fundamental and technical factors that could lead to bigger gains in the U.S. dollar which could send EUR/USD even lower in coming weeks.

EUR/USD seasonal performance since 2000 shows it has fallen in January for 16 of the past 25 years, or 64% of the time. Meanwhile in February it has dropped in 15 of the last 25 years. However, seasonality patterns should not be considered in isolation, they need to be corroborated with other factors.

The dollar has been underpinned by rising Treasury yields, putting the yen, sterling and euro under pressure near multi-month lows amid the shifting threat of tariffs. The greenback will tighten its stranglehold over global currency markets with little standing in the way of its remarkable run, and a significant number of foreign exchange forecasters polled by Reuters expect it to rise to parity with the euro in 2025.

If EUR/USD dives under the 1.0196 Fibonacci level, a 61.8% retrace of the 0.9528 to 1.1276 (2022-2023) EBS rise, that would expose 1.0000. Especially as the continued negative fourteen-week momentum reading points to bigger losses.

For more click on

data.title: China's Country Garden proposes offshore restructuring deal
data.description:
Chinese property developer Country Garden 2007 said on Thursday it proposed a restructuring deal for its offshore debt, providing its creditors multiple options, including converting their debt into cash and maturity extension.

The update on the offshore debt restructuring progress was provided ahead of Country Garden's liquidation hearing on Jan. 20, paving the way for the developer to seek more time from the Hong Kong High Court to implement a restructuring plan.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1122 GMT - The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's request for a label warning about Guillain-Barre syndrome risk on GSK's respiratory syncytial virus shot Arexvy could negatively impact vaccine sales, Bryan Garnier Research analysts say in a note. While a causal link between vaccination and GBS-- a rapid-onset muscle weakness caused by the immune system-- hasn't been proven, seven GBS cases per million Arexvy doses were observed, according to the FDA. "We believe the observed GBS incidence very close to natural GBS incidence in the non-vaccinated population," the analysts say. Arexvy sales already declined double-digit in 2H of 2024, following the narrowed recommendation to a smaller age range and more at-risk patients, the analysts say. Shares fall 0.04% at 1359.50 pence.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1109 GMT - Swatch Group could continue to face structural challenges this year, RBC Capital Markets analysts Piral Dadhania and Richard Chamberlain say in a note. The Swiss watchmaker has been facing a tough period, as it heavily relies on lower-income clients, which have reduced their luxury budget. Furthermore, the company's entry price brands Swatch, Tissot and Certina are threatened by the adoption of smartwatches, with prices overlapping. "We see further downside in 2025," the analysts say, adding that consensus estimates in the short term seem optimistic. RBC Capital Markets downgrades Swatch to underperform from sector perform. Shares are down 3% at 157.40 Swiss francs. The stock has dropped more than 28% in the past 12 months. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1108 GMT - European semiconductor share prices this year will mainly depend on investors' confidence in 2026 revenue and earnings growth prospects, Jefferies analysts write in a research note. They note that demand in major end-markets like PCs, smartphones, general servers, consumer electronics and networking remains weak. "We believe the weaker than previously expected outlook for 2025 is largely priced in for most European semiconductor stocks," the analysts say, pointing to 2026 as a more important year for the sector. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

1102 GMT - CVC Capital Partners has limited room to surprise this year after a strong share performance since its initial public offering in April, Barclays's Michael Sanderson and Krystal Han say in a research note. The Amsterdam-listed buyout group is set to continue raising investors' funds, but it will take time before that translates into revenue generation, the analysts say. Moreover, consensus expectations for 2025 already point to a sharp step up in CVC's carried interest and performance fees, the analysts add. Barclays lowers its recommendation on the stock to equalweight from overweight and increases its target price to 22 euros from 21.80 euros. Shares fall 0.2% to 21.32 euros. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1053 GMT - Talabat Holding's business is still growing, with many growth opportunities looking ahead, Barclays analysts write in a note. Delivery Hero's Middle East subsidiary Talabat has strong leadership positions across several markets, supported by a well-executed strategy, the analyst say. "Beyond restaurants, it has grocery at 24% gross merchandise value and broader retail at 1% GMV driving incremental users and frequency," they add. Talabat's business is very profitable, with further margin expansion potential, they say. The U.K. bank initiate its stock recommendation at overweight and a target price at 2.0 UAE dirham. Shares are down 0.6% at 1.63 dirham. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1049 GMT - Indian shares ended lower, weighed by bank and tech stocks. The earnings season kicked off today. HDFC Bank dropped 1.6% and Axis Bank was 1.25% lower. Tata Consultancy Services declined 1.7% ahead of its results released after market close. TCS reported higher third-quarter revenue and net profit. Tech Mahindra fell 1.1%. Tata Steel was the biggest loser on the index, dropping 2.1%. Consumer stocks rose, with Nestle India up 1.9% and Hindustan Unilever gaining 1.5%. The benchmark Sensex fell 0.7% to 77620.21. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

1044 GMT - Repsol shares look set to rise as refining margins rebound, UBS analysts write. The Spanish energy major's stock has fallen 12% in the past six months and investors fail to account for higher gas prices and Repsol's earnings momentum, the analysts write. The analysts increase their earnings per share forecasts by 17%, putting them 8% ahead of consensus expectations. Repsol shares could be boosted by buybacks worth around 800 million euros in 2025, a UBS forecast that is nearly 150 million euros ahead of consensus expectations. A strong balance sheet and asset sales across should also boost shares, they write. UBS upgrades the stock's rating to buy from neutral, and increases its target price to 13 euros from 11.5 euros. Shares trade up 2.1% at 12.04 euros.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

1032 GMT - Marks & Spencer's outlook comments chime with the current bleak mood around the U.K.'s economic prospects, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould says in a note. The U.K. retailer said that the external environment remains challenging and that it expects the business to face higher costs from increases in taxation. Last October, in her first budget as U.K. Treasury chief, Rachel Reeves increased the national living wage and hiked employers' national insurance contributions, implying extra costs for companies. "The storm clouds have certainly darkened," Mould says. Shares in M&S trade 6.3% lower, while those of other U.K. retailers including Tesco and B&M European Value Retail fall 1.5% and 13%, respectively. "Turmoil in the markets implies a massive loss of confidence in the U.K. government," Mould says. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1019 GMT - Partners Group Holding looks set to benefit from its exposure to the U.S., where opportunities in both investment exits and fundraising are expected to arise this year, Barclays's Michael Sanderson and Krystal Han say in a research note. "We see Partners Group as the best positioned in our private capital coverage to benefit from likely 2025 conditions and we upgrade our rating to [overweight]" from equalweight, the analysts say. Shares in the Swiss private-equity group could rise as higher transactional activity provides evidence of an improvement in conditions, the analysts add. Barclays also lifts its target price on Partners Group to 1,470 Swiss francs from 1,120 francs. Shares rise 1.8% to 1,321 francs. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

1016 GMT - Telecommunication companies are well-placed for another positive year in 2025, Morgan Stanley analysts write in a note. The sector showed its strength last year, outperforming the broader market, the analysts say. "Over the previous 10 years, the sector was a consistent underperformer, with only three years of outperformance," they add. However, resilient pricing, declining capital expenses and the return of M&A activity will likely support momentum, the U.S. bank says. Shares in Deutsche Telekom, Swisscom, and BT are expected to perform well in 2025, though French telcos such as Orange and Bouygues look less attractive, they add. BT and Deutsche Telekom shares are up 0.8% at 144.90 pence and 0.5% at 29.24 euros, respectively. Swisscom shares are flat at 508 Swiss francs. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

1016 GMT - Marks & Spencer reported a strong performance for the third quarter, but the absence of a guidance upgrade could disappoint, Peel Hunt analysts say in a note. The U.K. retailer booked sales of 4.06 billion pounds for the 13 weeks to Dec. 28, 5.6% higher than in the year-earlier period. The food business remains stellar, while the clothing, home and beauty segment is also solid, they say. Despite the positive results, the company continues to expect a challenging environment due to inflation, interest rates and higher costs from increases in taxation. Consumer behavior is uncertain and there are still three months of the fiscal year to go, the analysts say, adding that the company will face headwinds next year, including expected higher national insurance contributions. Shares are down 5.1% at 358 pence. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1011 GMT - Tesco's share-price drop after reporting 3Q results could be seen as a modest decline and a win for the grocer, given the wider pressures on the sector and the company's strong market-share gains, AJ Bell investment director Russ Mould writes. The lack of guidance upgrade despite sales growth could be the outcome of Tesco's commitment to keeping prices low and having extra staff during Christmas season to help shoppers and build brand loyalty, he adds. The decision means lower profits, but it could be part of a strategy of foregoing a short-term boost to benefit from an improved competitive position in the long term. "Investors may have been disappointed at the decline in revenue growth, but this reflected lower levels of inflation." Shares are down 1.8% at 36.33 pounds. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: FDA Label Warning Threatens GSK's RSV Vaccine Sales — Market Talk
data.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration's request for a label warning about Guillain-Barre syndrome risk on GSK's respiratory syncytial virus shot Arexvy could negatively impact vaccine sales, Bryan Garnier Research analysts say in a note. While a causal link between vaccination and GBS-- a rapid-onset muscle weakness caused by the immune system-- hasn't been proven, seven GBS cases per million Arexvy doses were observed, according to the FDA. "We believe the observed GBS incidence very close to natural GBS incidence in the non-vaccinated population," the analysts say. Arexvy sales already declined double-digit in 2H of 2024, following the narrowed recommendation to a smaller age range and more at-risk patients, the analysts say. Shares fall 0.04% at 1359.50 pence.(helena.smolak@wsj.com)

data.title: UK Gilt Sell-Off Hits Sterling, Says Mitsubishi UFG
data.description:
Sterling has come under more selling pressure over the last 24 hours with GBPUSD falling below the April low and EURGBP rising to its highest levels since early November, said Mitsubishi UFG.

Sterling weakness reflects more investor unease over the ongoing sell-off in the United Kingdom Gilt market which has captured more attention this week after the 10-year and 30-year Gilt yields rose to their highest levels since October 2008 and August 1998 respectively, wrote the bank in a note to clients. It has drawn some comparisons with the sell-off following former Prime Minister Liz Truss' ill-fated mini-budget in fall 2022.

The scale of the sell-off is currently significantly smaller, stated MUFG. The 30-year Gilt yield increased by around 300bps between the lows in August 2022 and from highs in September 2022.

The recent move higher in Gilt yields also appears broadly in line with the similar moves higher in other bond markets. The 10-year and 30-year Gilt yields have increased by around 55bps and 60bps respectively since the end of November.

In comparison, the 10-year and 30-year United States Treasury yields have increased by around 50bps and 55bps respectively. Similarly, 10-year and 30-year German government bond yields have increased by around 45bps. It would suggest that the Gilt market sell-off is mainly driven by the broader repricing of long-end bonds as investors seek more compensation for taking on duration risk rather than UK-specific factors at the current juncture, pointed out the bank.

Having said that the rising cost of UK government borrowing if sustained will put pressure on the government to tighten fiscal policy, added MUFG. The UK press reported Thursday that Finance Minister Rachel Reeves is already drawing up contingency plans for emergency spending cuts or tax rises. Treasury sources have reportedly acknowledged that the government could be forced to act as soon as March in response to higher borrowing costs.

The Office for Budget Responsibility will present updated economic and fiscal forecasts in March and if it shows that the government risks breaking the self-imposed fiscal rules then the finance minister would be more inclined to take corrective action rather than wait until the Fall Budget, according to the bank. Reeves is reportedly against further tax rises or borrowing and will cut spending if unfavorable market conditions continue.

In last Fall's Budget, the government highlighted that it only had fiscal headroom of 9.9 billion pounds which could be wiped out by higher borrowing costs. The Fall Budget included front-loaded fiscal spending which is expected to be supportive for growth this year which is adding to unease amongst Gilt investors alongside the persistence of more elevated core and services inflation in the UK.

Higher yields in the UK and the Bank of England's caution over cutting rates were one of the key reasons why sterling outperformed last year when it was the second-best performing G10 currency after the US dollar, noted MUFG. Recent price action highlights though that higher yields aren't always positive for a currency if they are driven by unease over the public finances and inflation in the UK.

The BoE could also come under pressure to scale back its quantitative tightening program if Gilt yields continue to rise. The BoE is currently committed to shrinking its Gilt holdings by a further 100 billion pounds in the year from October of which around 12 billion pounds will be through active Gilt sales and the rest by allowing Gilts to mature.

Overall, the unfavorable market developments have increased downside risks for sterling at the start of this year and increased the likelihood of a fall back below 1.2000, said the bank.

data.title: Photocat Enlists Essity to Distribute Dust-suppression Product
data.description:
Green technology company Photocat PPCAT plans to sign up Essity (ESSITY-A.ST, ESSITY-B.ST) as a distribution and commercial partner for its sustainable dust-suppression product InnoLig+.

The Swedish consumer goods company will be responsible for distributing the product in key European markets including the Nordics, Germany, Poland, Baltics, Switzerland, Spain and the UK, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Portugal Trade Gap Widens in November
data.description:
Portugal's trade deficit widened to €2.502 billion in November 2024 from €2.434 billion in the same month last year, as exports fell while imports increased.

Exports fell by 1.8% year-on-year to €6.796 billion, due to lower sales of transport equipment and parts (-10%), and capital goods (-7.7%).

On the other hand, exports of industrial supplies went up 1.9%.

Among major trade partners, exports fell to France (-10.7%), the UK (-13.6%) and Italy (-14.4%).

Meanwhile, imports increased by 4.7% year-on-year to €9.298 billion, mainly due to higher purchases of industrial supplies (23.6%) and consumer goods (7.7%).

Imports rose from Netherlands (3.2%), but fell from Spain (-2.2%), Germany (-3.9%), and France (-7.3%).

data.title: Market Chatter: Azul, Gol Parent Expected to Sign Memorandum of Understanding for Potential Deal
data.description:
Azul AZUL and Abra, the parent company of Gol, are expected to sign a memorandum of understanding for the potential merger of the two Brazilian airlines, news outlet Valor Economico reported Wednesday.

The objective of the memorandum is to discuss details that could pertain to the deal, the report said.

Azul and Gol did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' requests for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Q Technology Posts 11% Drop in December 2024 Camera Module Sales Volume; Fingerprint Recognition Module Sales Volume Up 96%
data.description:
Q Technology (Group) 1478 recorded a 10.7% year-over-year decrease in the total sales volume of camera modules to roughly 32.0 million units in December 2024, according to a Thursday bourse filing.

The sales volume of fingerprint recognition modules was around 20.4 million during the reporting month, up 95.8% from a year prior, the camera modules technology company said.

data.title: Red-Hot Singapore Index Has Room to Run on Global Uncertainties — Analysis
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

Singapore's benchmark equities index is fresh off a record high, helped by a resurgence in the city's heavyweight bank stocks in a time of higher-for-longer global interest rates. New global uncertainties and local measures to boost trading liquidity could add to the gains, analysts said.

The FTSE Straits Times Index notched its highest-ever close Wednesday. Even after a step back on Thursday, the index is up about 21% over the past 12 months, trailing only a chip- and artificial intelligence-fueled rally in Taiwan's Taiex among major Asian markets.

Much of the gains reflect the outperformance in Singapore's financial sector, with banks rising in an environment of high rates that has boosted net interest income. DBS, Southeast Asia's largest bank by assets, has climbed more than 50% over the past 12 months, while OCBC and UOB logged gains of more than 30% each. All three banks hit new highs this week.

Going forward, stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data justifies more patience in the Fed's rate-cutting path, with market recalibration for high-for-longer rates driving a more favorable backdrop for the local banks' net interest income ahead, IG market strategist Yeap Jun Rong said.

Singapore could also benefit from the return of a Trump administration in the U.S. and heightened uncertainties about new policies in global trade and other areas.

Without more clarity on new U.S. policies, investors could shift equity allocations more defensively this year, favoring safe havens such as Singapore, Morgan Stanley analysts wrote in a note.

"Singapore tends to outperform during bear markets in Asia/EM when historically it has delivered more positive returns than most other markets," they said.

OCBC Investment Research analysts flagged the possibility of more challenges and volatility "if Trump 1.0 is any indication," saying in a research report that the coming year will likely be dominated by the "triple Ts" of Trump, tariffs and trade.

Another factor this year is Singapore's efforts to beef up the local equities markets, with a central bank report on strengthening equities markets due in August and with possible updates and recommendations coming in phases before then.

"The combination of seemingly stronger political will and low market expectations drives our conviction that soon-to-be-announced initiatives will likely have a meaningfully positive market impact, even if their exact details are still to be fleshed out," Morgan Stanley analysts said.

The new measures are likely to focus on improving stock-trading liquidity, through an injection of capital, and "valuation multiples could rise as much as 20% to narrow the gap to global peers," the analysts said.

Still, the biggest focus in 2025 will be on the U.S. economy and the decisions of the Federal Reserve.

"A more inflationary environment paired with resilient U.S. economic data could reiterate a gradual pace of Fed rate cuts, positioning banks as a top choice for investors seeking strong earnings growth," said IG's Yeap.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: NZDUSD Technical Analysis – The greenback remains in the driving seat
data.description:
Fundamental Overview

The USD got a boost recently from another set of hot data as the US Job Openings surprised to the upside and the prices paid index in the ISM Services PMI jumped to the highest level since 2023.

The market’s pricing didn’t change much though and Fed’s Waller yesterday kept the rate cuts hopes alive saying that the pace will be driven by inflation progress. Since the central bank has switched its focus back to inflation, the US CPI report next week should have a bigger influence on interest rates expectations than the US NFP tomorrow (barring big deviations).

On the NZD side, the RBNZ cut interest rates by 50 bps as expected at the last meeting. We haven’t got any fresh New Zealand data in the meantime, but the market continues to price in a 76% chance of a 50 bps cut in February and a total of 124 bps of easing by year end.

NZDUSD Technical Analysis – Daily Timeframe

NZDUSD Daily

On the daily chart, we can see that NZDUSD is consolidating near the recent lows with the 2022 low around the 0.5511 level now being very close. We have a downward trendline defining the bearish momentum. The sellers will likely continue to lean on it to position for further downside, while the buyers will look for a break higher to start targeting new highs.

NZDUSD Technical Analysis – 4 hour Timeframe

NZDUSD 4 hour

On the 4 hour chart, there’s not much else we can add here as the price action remains mostly rangebound as we continue to wait for the US NFP and CPI reports. The resistance zone around the 0.5670 level will be a key level to look at if we get there in the next days.

NZDUSD Technical Analysis – 1 hour Timeframe

NZDUSD 1 hour

On the 1 hour chart, we can see that the price is trading at the support zone around the 0.5590 level. The buyers will likely step in here to position for the rally into the resistance zone, while the sellers will look for the price to stay below the support to keep pushing towards the 2022 lows. The red lines define the average daily range for today.

Upcoming Catalysts

Tomorrow, we conclude the week with the US NFP report.

data.title: Copper driven higher by technical factors
data.description:
Copper prices extended gains for a sixth session on Thursday as technical factors provided momentum and outweighed concern over U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's tariff plans for top metals consumer China.

Three-month copper HG1! on the London Metal Exchange (LME) rose 0.7% to $9,096 a metric ton by 1049 GMT after hitting its highest since Dec. 16 at $9,099.

The metal used in power and construction continues to recover from a five-month low of $8,757 touched on Dec. 31. This week brought support on the technical front as it broke above resistance from the 21-day moving average, which now supports at a major psychological level around $9,000.

The market, however, remains worried about how Trump will handle tariff policy after his return to the White House on Jan 20. During the election campaign Trump vowed to impose tariffs of 60% on Chinese imports, but there have since been conflicting reports on the level of potential tariffs.

CNN on Wednesday reported that Trump is considering declaring a national economic emergency to provide legal justification for a series of universal tariffs on allies and adversaries. On Monday the Washington Post said Trump was looking at more nuanced tariffs, which he later denied.

The Chinese yuan, meanwhile, has been hovering around a 16-month low, prompting some Chinese traders to buy copper to try to shield themselves from this weakness and uncertainty about the future, said Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank.

The Yangshan copper premium (SMM-CUYP-CN), which reflects demand for copper imported into China, reached its highest in more than a year at $73 a ton, against $43 two months ago.

In other metals, LME aluminium (CMAl3) rose 1.5% to $2,537 a ton, zinc ZNC1! added 1.4% to $2,864.50, lead LEAD1! gained 0.2% to $1,942, tin FTIN1! edged up 0.2% to $30,110 and nickel NICKEL1! was steady at $15,440.

data.title: UAE Stocks Mixed as Fed Signals Cautious Rate Cuts
data.description:
Emirati stocks closed Thursday's trading session mixed amid concerns over US economic policies under President-elect Donald Trump, including proposed tariffs and immigration measures.

At closing, the FTSE ADX General Index fell 0.033% and the DFM General Index added 0.384%.

Minutes from the Federal Open Market Committee's December 2024 meeting released Wednesday revealed an overall cautious stance on rate cuts, with inflation concerns tied to Trump's incoming policies. Still, Fed Governor Christopher Waller expressed optimism, predicting inflation will ease in the coming months to support the case for another 25-basis-point rate cut in March 2025.

"Altogether, Governor Waller said that this view of inflation and his expectation that the labor market has stabilized supports further reductions in the Fed's policy rate in 2025, though he said the pace, timing, and magnitude of cuts will ultimately be data-dependent," Morgan Stanley said in a note.

In corporate news, NMDC Energy (ADX:NMDCENR) bagged a $1.14 billion subsea gas pipeline installation and onshore engineering and construction contract for the second phase of Taiwan Power Co.'s Tung-Hsiao power plant renewal project. The energy industry-focused engineering, procurement, and construction company ended trading 2.45% higher.

In Dubai, Al Salam Bank (DFM:SALAM_BAH) fell 0.513% after it announced a partnership with Y K Almoayyed & Sons' electronics and home appliances division to offer exclusive benefits to customers. The offer will run until Oct. 26.
